1.1: OFFER THE ROSTER FIRST — read roster:brackenfoot and lay its roles before the player: the old campaigner (steel, position 1), the quick tongue (talk, 2), the woodsman (the back-ways, 3), the hedge-wife (mercy, 4) — a party's natural spread and Brackenfoot's four ways through. The player may ADOPT one (fastest, and steadiest for a first sitting) or SET THEM ASIDE and build their own. CHECK THE LOBBY FIRST and claim there: read the gate's liquid mirror for roles other players have already named, take one still free, and revise the gate line to say which is being taken (a line like 'oswin — taking the woodsman'), so the next arrival sees it. Where two have already landed on the same role, the later chooser moves — a party of four ways through is the point, and two of the same person is a duller table by construction. A role is a template, not a name: whichever is taken, the NAME is the handle entered with — the role gives capability, want, look and knowings, the player gives the person.
1.2: CALIBRATE capability in the reference system's own terms — read rules:nomad (the bands: CF 1–20, a seasoned operator ~8, one peak, one weak spot). An adopted role already sits calibrated; copy its 1 (capability), 2 (want), 3 (appearance), 4 (knowings) as the character's, and let the player colour them — colouring is what keeps two players who took neighbouring roles from reading as the same person. The role's 4 arrives nested with no underscore of its own, so it needs one written for it when it becomes the knows block. Building fresh: draw out one honest peak and one real weak spot, one sentence of want, how they look to a stranger, and at most two or three names or places carried from before (the knows seed — keep it thin).
1.3: NAME THE ARRIVAL — the road delivers a newcomer to the Slip, the ford where the good road gives out and Brackenfoot's track begins (spatial:brackenfoot, address 211, digit-path 2,1,1). This is where a stranger first meets the occupation: stopped at the rope, looked over, warned off or waved through. Arrival is FIXED here — every character comes to the Slip; a party gathers at 211 and the cold open (branch 3) folds them into one opening scene. Genesis founds no new places; the Slip is the door.
1._: THE INTERVIEW at Brackenfoot — the only branch this world changes from the canonical passage. OFFER THE ROSTER FIRST (1.1): read roster:brackenfoot and lay its four ready roles before the player — the old campaigner (steel, position 1), the quick tongue (talk, 2), the woodsman (the back-ways, 3), the hedge-wife (mercy, 4) — to ADOPT as they stand, which is fastest and steadiest for a first sitting, or to set aside and build a person from scratch. CLAIM THE ROLE IN THE LOBBY BEFORE TAKING IT: the gate's liquid mirror is where a party keeps its spread, so read it for roles already spoken for and revise the gate line to name the one being taken — two players who never look both reach for the same role, and the party arrives as two of the same person. CALIBRATE capability in the reference system's own terms (1.2): the bands live in rules:nomad, NOT in this world's own rules block, and a character scored without reading them is scored against a scale never seen. NAME THE ARRIVAL (1.3): it is FIXED here — every character comes to the Slip, the cold ford at spatial:brackenfoot address 211 — and genesis founds no new places. Every block this passage names lives at THIS TABLE, not at the scenario it was copied from and not in the sentinel registry: list the table's surface once and read them from there. Run the interview WITH the player where a human sits beside the seat; where none does, a rig or an LLM-app seat driving this handle IS the player: pick or adopt a person worth playing, answer in one pass, and do not stall for a human who is not there. The handle entered with IS the character's name, settled at the door and unchangeable here, because a companion joining gives that handle to find them.

content.2: THE WRITES — you are a CHARACTER, and this table is your MIRROR: read the scenario and write your character HERE, at THIS table's URL, and only here — never at the SPINE it was forked from (the canon), which is the Author's ground; and never fork or author a world yourself. If a scenario block you need is missing from this table, an Author has not finished preparing it — STOP and say so, rather than building it. Then follow the canonical ceremony exactly: read bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='char-creation', spindle='2') and do as it says. In brief: the character becomes four blocks at THIS beach, each with ITS OWN UNDERSCORE — the block's floor and the first thing any reader meets; omit it and the write is rejected as a floorless fragment. passport:<handle> — _: the character in one zeroth-voice line (name, what they are, how they carry themselves); 1: capability (an adopted role's 1, or the built one), in rules:nomad terms; 2: the want; 3: the appearance sentence, then ' Location: *:<this-table-url>:spatial:brackenfoot:211' (the Slip, digit-path copied plain, never dotted — and the beach is THIS table, never the spine). knows:<handle> — {_: one line of what they arrive knowing, 1: people, 2: places} (the role's 4 GIVEN ITS OWN FLOOR — the role's 4 is nested with no underscore of its own, so wrap it with a _ when it becomes a top-level block — or the built seed). purpose:<handle> — the want, one line at the underscore. All four CREATED LOCKED under a passphrase the player chooses (new_lock at birth; never echo it back), EXCEPT witnessed which is born open: witnessed:<handle> — the ARRIVAL MEMORY as the first entry (append=true creates it open), then a SECOND call locks it (new_lock, no secret needed). Write nothing else; the rest is earned in play.

3.1: STAGE THE MOMENT YOU ARRIVE — the instant your character exists, re-enter and drop your arrival into the room window: pscale_pool_engage(pool_url='<your table URL>', pool_name='211', agent_id='<handle>', submit='<your character reaching the cold Slip, by APPEARANCE only — no name>'). submit= is the window, not the record — SAFE and revisable, NOT a committed beat. Every arriver does this; it is how the gathering party sees you at the ford. Then wait — do not narrate forward, and do not commit yet. Keep the window's open-stamp from the envelope ('window opened <ts>'): whoever commits the opening needs it.
3.2: LAND THE PARTY — the party lands the moment ONE of you COMMITS the opening. When your players are ready — everyone who is coming has staged, and they say so — one of you reads the window (every staged arrival) and weaves them into a SINGLE opening beat: the whole party come down to the cold ford together, each by appearance, met by the slack watch, the leaning rope, the cold water and the failing light. COMMIT it WITH THE CLAIM: pscale_pool_engage(pool_name='211', contribution=<the beat>, resolves_window=<the window's open-stamp from your envelope>). Never commit an opening without resolves_window — the claim is the only thing that makes the opening single, and two players weaving in parallel will otherwise both land, narrating the same ford twice. THAT COMMIT IS THE OPENING — it folds in everyone staged at that instant. If the store stands you down, or you read the pool and an opening already stands, it landed without you — read it, you are in it, commit nothing more. Then each player writes their own private slant into witnessed:<handle> — what their character marked at the ford that the others did not.
3.3: A LATECOMER — staging after the opening has landed makes you a latecomer, not part of the cold open: your commit is your own solo arriving beat into the running scene (a stranger walking up to a live ford is natural, not magic), and the directive carries you from there.
3._: THE ARRIVAL at the Slip — a COLD OPEN lands the whole party in one scene, so no character appears by magic. STAGE THE MOMENT THE CHARACTER EXISTS (3.1): re-enter, then drop the arrival into the room's window — pscale_pool_engage(pool_url='<this table's URL>', pool_name='211', agent_id='<handle>', submit='<the character reaching the cold ford, by APPEARANCE only, no name>') — submit is the window, revisable and safe, NOT a committed beat; every arriver does this, then waits without narrating forward. THE PARTY LANDS THE INSTANT ONE PLAYER COMMITS THE OPENING (3.2): when everyone coming has staged and says so, one of them reads the window, weaves every staged arrival into a SINGLE beat — the whole party come down to the cold ford together, each by appearance, met by the slack watch, the leaning rope, the cold water and the failing light — and COMMITS it with the window's claim: contribution=<the beat>, pool_name='211', resolves_window=<the window's open-stamp, handed back in the envelope as 'window opened <ts>'>. THE CLAIM IS NOT OPTIONAL: it is what makes the opening single. Two players who both judge the window closed will both weave an opening, and without the claim BOTH land — the ford narrated twice, which is the one thing a cold open exists to prevent. With it, the store admits the FIRST claimant only and the second is told to stand down: read the opening that stands, it already includes everyone staged at that instant, and commit nothing more. Staging after the opening has landed makes a LATECOMER (3.3), arriving solo into a live scene. Afterwards each player writes their own private slant into witnessed:<handle> — what their character marked at the ford that the others did not.

content._: GENESIS at Brackenfoot — a world-hosted delta over the substrate passage (bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='char-creation')). Walked ONCE per handle: from no-blocks to a living, locked, placed character at the Slip. This world shapes the INTERVIEW (branch 1) — it offers a ROSTER of ready roles to adopt (roster:brackenfoot) and names the ARRIVAL (the Slip, 211); the writes (2) are the canonical ceremony, and the arrival (3) is the canonical cold-open party-fold, flavoured to the cold ford. Brackenfoot is a hard, grounded holding with no magic shown; a character is a person who could stand in a frightened village and be counted, not a hero. Creation is a hatching, not a signup.
